intro:
Tomorrow we are closed.
You can request repeat prescriptions for chronic medication 24 hours a day (including weekends) in 2 ways:
patientenomgeving: You can request repeat prescriptions online via the patient environment on the website. You must first register. Please note that after creating an account it can take up to 7 days before the account is active.
receptenlijn: Call your own GP's practice phone number and select the prescription line option on the menu.
receptenlijnVragen: Name and date of birth of the person the prescription is for
Street and house number of your home address
Name or names of the medications you are ordering (have the boxes at hand!)
welkeMedicijnen:
You can only request prescriptions for medications that you use on the advice of your GP or specialist chronically (usually daily).

If you want to request medication for newly occurring complaints or temporary medications -- that you received for a specific period -- you cannot order these medications via the prescription line or website. For this you can call the practice assistant during office hours, and if necessary your GP will be consulted about your request.
wanneerKlaar: If you call the prescription line or order a prescription via the website before 12:00 noon, you can collect your medications from your pharmacy 2 working days later (from 15:00 they will be ready).
wanneerKlaarVoorbeelden: If you call/email on Tuesday evening, your medications will be ready at the pharmacy on Friday.
If you call/email on Friday afternoon after 12:00 or during the weekend, you can collect your medications on Wednesday after 15:00.
